steps.title: Test each transition in the sentence
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'that is,': Means "in other words" — signals that the second clause restates or clarifies the first. The first clause says cheeses are valued for "regional authenticity" and the second explains what that means (local ingredients and methods). ✓
  • Choice B — 'still,': Signals something unexpected or concessive — there is no contrast or surprise between the two clauses. ✗
  • Choice C — 'rather,': Signals a correction or substitution of one idea for another — the second clause does not replace the first, it elaborates on it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'then,': Signals sequence or consequence — the second clause is not a result or next step, it is a definition. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the logical relationship
steps.content: The second clause defines "regional authenticity" from the first clause — this is a clarification relationship, and "that is," is the standard signal for clarification. That matches choice A — A is correct.
